questions.question: Explain the primary role of the Operating System as a resource manager.
keyPoints: Manages hardware resources like CPU, memory, disk, and I/O devices.
Acts as an intermediary between user applications and the computer hardware.
Allocates resources to processes efficiently and fairly to prevent conflicts.
Provides a stable and secure environment for program execution.

questions.question: Describe the key differences between a monolithic kernel and a microkernel architecture.
keyPoints: Monolithic kernels have all OS services (scheduling, file system, drivers) in the kernel space, leading to performance efficiency but less modularity.
Microkernels minimize the kernel, running most services (file system, drivers) as user-level processes, improving stability and security.
Monolithic designs are faster due to system calls within the kernel, while microkernels use message passing, which can be slower but more reliable.
Microkernels are easier to extend and maintain because services can be updated or replaced without modifying the core kernel.

questions.question: List two primary functions of an operating system related to resource management.
keyPoints: Process Management: Creating, scheduling, and terminating processes; handling CPU allocation.
Memory Management: Allocating and deallocating memory space to processes; managing virtual memory.
